sarcoidosis.teachingPoint: Noncaseating granulomas with perilymphatic distribution are characteristic of sarcoidosis. Look for asteroid bodies and Schaumann bodies within giant cells.

tuberculosis.teachingPoint: Caseating granulomas with central necrosis are characteristic of tuberculosis. AFB stain may reveal acid-fast bacilli.

histoplasmosis.teachingPoint: Small intracellular yeast forms with narrow-based budding are characteristic of histoplasmosis. GMS stain highlights the yeast forms.

blastomycosis.teachingPoint: Large yeast with broad-based budding are pathognomonic for blastomycosis. Look for thick refractile walls.

coccidioidomycosis.teachingPoint: Spherules containing endospores are pathognomonic for coccidioidomycosis. Don't confuse with Blastomyces.

gpa.teachingPoint: Necrotizing granulomas with vasculitis are characteristic of granulomatosis with polyangiitis (GPA). Look for geographic necrosis and leukocytoclastic vasculitis.

foreign_body.teachingPoint: Foreign body granulomas with polarizable material indicate exogenous substance exposure. Look for refractile material under polarized light.

patterns.description: delicate papillary architecture with fibrovascular cores
preserved polarity and minimal cytologic atypia

papillary_urothelial_neoplasm_of_low_mal.teachingPoint: PUNLMP retains an orderly papillary architecture and minimal atypia, which is why it is separated from overt papillary urothelial carcinoma.

patterns.description: flat urothelial lesion with diffuse atypia
full-thickness nuclear atypia with loss of maturation

urothelial_carcinoma_in_situ.teachingPoint: Bladder CIS is a flat, high-grade lesion defined by diffuse urothelial atypia and loss of maturation rather than papillary architecture.

bcg_induced_granulomatous_cystitis.teachingPoint: Intravesical BCG can cause granulomatous cystitis that becomes a major mimic on bladder biopsies and TURBT specimens.

patterns.description: endophytic urothelial growth with an orderly nested pattern

inverted_urothelial_papilloma.teachingPoint: Inverted urothelial papilloma is a benign endophytic lesion and helps frame the difference between orderly nested growth and infiltrative carcinoma.

patterns.description: Michaelis-Gutmann bodies in von Hansemann histiocytes

malakoplakia.teachingPoint: Malakoplakia is a classic urinary-tract inflammatory mimic characterized by histiocytic sheets and Michaelis-Gutmann bodies.

stainRoles.PAS: highlight fungal cell walls and mucin

difficultyLevels.beginner: Classic presentations with obvious diagnostic features
difficultyLevels.intermediate: Moderate diagnostic complexity, requires pattern recognition
difficultyLevels.advanced: Subtle findings, mimics, or rare presentations
